rationale_overall: At the close of Q3 2025 all eight watchlist positions retain intact theses. NVDA reported record Q2 fiscal 2026 revenue of $46.74B (+56% YoY) on August 27, anchoring the AI bucket alongside MSFT and GOOGL. RKLB signed a 10-launch Synspective deal on September 22, bringing total contracted Electron launches to 21. IONQ completed the Oxford Ionics acquisition on September 17, deepening its trapped-ion roadmap, and agreed to acquire Vector Atomic for quantum sensing. CEG's Crane Clean Energy Center restart is ahead of schedule (reported September 23) and the June 3 Meta 20-year PPA reinforces the nuclear-utility-PPA thesis; NUKZ provides complementary uranium-miner and SMR-developer breadth. BOTZ's robotics thesis is intact. The one underrepresented named wave — defense/rearmament — has a structural catalyst (NATO 5% GDP commitment at The Hague Summit, June 25, 2025), but the profile flags defense primes have already re-rated sharply and no Q3 catalyst is strong enough to displace a current position. The watchlist retains its balance across AI (3), rockets (1), robotics (1), quantum (1), and nuclear (2).
